title: Container

remote_id.description: The third-party API ID of the matching object.

created_at.description: The datetime that this object was created by Merge.

modified_at.description: The datetime that this object was modified by Merge.

status.description:
The container's status.

* `DRAFT` - DRAFT
* `PUBLISHED` - PUBLISHED
* `ARCHIVED` - ARCHIVED
* `TRASH` - TRASH

container_url.description: The URL to the webpage of the container.

type.description:
The container's type.

* `FOLDER` - FOLDER
* `SPACE` - SPACE
* `COLLECTION` - COLLECTION
* `SECTION` - SECTION
* `CATEGORY` - CATEGORY
* `DATABASE` - DATABASE

visibility.description:
The container's visibility.

* `PUBLIC` - PUBLIC
* `INTERNAL` - INTERNAL
* `RESTRICTED` - RESTRICTED

remote_created_at.description: When the third party's container was created.

remote_updated_at.description: When the third party's container was updated.

parent_article.description: The parent article a container is nested within.

parent_container.description: The parent container a container is nested within.

remote_was_deleted.description: Indicates whether or not this object has been deleted in the third party platform. Full coverage deletion detection is a premium add-on. Native deletion detection is offered for free with limited coverage. [Learn more](https://docs.merge.dev/integrations/hris/supported-features/).

description:
# The Container Object
### Description
The Container object is used to represent a grouping of articles in the knowledge base. This can include Spaces, Folders, Databases, etc.
### Usage Example
Fetch from the `GET /api/knowledgebase/containers` endpoint and view their containers.
